docs.title: Inventory API
docs.text: Cette documentation présente une API REST pédagogique construite avec FastAPI.

docs.title: Objectifs
docs.text:
• Illustrer la structuration d'un projet Python maintenable.
  • Appliquer les conventions de docstrings PEP 257.
  • Exposer automatiquement une documentation technique avec MkDocs.
  • Tester une API REST avec TestClient.

docs.title: Endpoints disponibles
docs.text: Méthode URL Rôle GET / Vérifier que l'application répond POST /products Créer un produit GET /products Lister les produits GET /products/{product_id} Consulter un produit

docs.title: Créer l'environnement virtuel
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

Sous Windows PowerShell :

python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

docs.title: Installer le projet
docs.text:
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Lancer l'API
docs.text:
uvicorn inventory_api.main:app --reload

L'API est alors disponible sur http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Lancer les tests
docs.text: pytest

docs.title: Générer la documentation
docs.text:
mkdocs serve # (1)
mkdocs build
  1. Utilisé pour servir la documentation sur une URL

docs.title: Référence API Python
docs.text: Cette page est générée à partir des docstrings du code source.

docs.title: inventory_api.models
docs.text:
Data models used by the Inventory API.

The module contains Pydantic models shared by the API routes and the business service layer.

docs.title: Product
docs.text:
Bases: ProductCreate

Represent a product returned by the API.

Attributes:

Name Type Description id int

Unique technical identifier generated by the application.

name str

Human-readable product name.

price float

Positive product price expressed in euros.

Source code in src\inventory_api\models.py 
class Product(ProductCreate):
    """Represent a product returned by the API.

    Attributes:
        id: Unique technical identifier generated by the application.
        name: Human-readable product name.
        price: Positive product price expressed in euros.
    """

    id: int = Field(
        examples=[1],
        description="Unique product identifier.",
    )

docs.title: ProductCreate
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Represent the payload required to create a product.

Attributes:

Name Type Description name str

Human-readable product name.

price float

Positive product price expressed in euros.

Source code in src\inventory_api\models.py 
class ProductCreate(BaseModel):
    """Represent the payload required to create a product.

    Attributes:
        name: Human-readable product name.
        price: Positive product price expressed in euros.
    """

    name: str = Field(
        min_length=2,
        max_length=80,
        examples=["Sauce Labs Backpack"],
        description="Product name between 2 and 80 characters.",
    )
    price: float = Field(
        gt=0,
        examples=[29.99],
        description="Strictly positive product price.",
    )

docs.title: inventory_api.services.product_service
docs.text: Business service for product management.

docs.title: ProductService
docs.text:
Manage products in memory.

This class intentionally stores data in memory to keep the exercise focused on documentation, API structure and tests. A real project would replace this implementation with a repository connected to a database.

Source code in src\inventory_api\services\product_service.py 
class ProductService:
    """Manage products in memory.

    This class intentionally stores data in memory to keep the exercise focused
    on documentation, API structure and tests. A real project would replace this
    implementation with a repository connected to a database.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        """Initialize an empty product store."""
        self._products: dict[int, Product] = {}
        self._next_id = 1

    def create(self, payload: ProductCreate) -> Product:
        """Create a product from a validated payload.

        Args:
            payload: Product data received from the API layer.

        Returns:
            The product enriched with a generated identifier.
        """
        product = Product(id=self._next_id, **payload.model_dump())
        self._products[product.id] = product
        self._next_id += 1
        return product

    def list_all(self) -> list[Product]:
        """Return all existing products.

        Returns:
            Products currently stored by the service.
        """
        return list(self._products.values())

    def get_by_id(self, product_id: int) -> Product | None:
        """Return one product by identifier.

        Args:
            product_id: Identifier of the requested product.

        Returns:
            The matching product, or ``None`` when no product exists.
        """
        return self._products.get(product_id)

    def clear(self) -> None:
        """Remove all products and reset the identifier sequence.

        This helper is useful for deterministic automated tests.
        """
        self._products.clear()
        self._next_id = 1

docs.title: inventory_api.routers.products
docs.text: HTTP routes dedicated to product resources.

docs.title: create_product(payload)
docs.text:
Create and return a new product.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default payload ProductCreate

Product creation payload.

required

Returns:

Type Description Product

The created product.

Source code in src\inventory_api\routers\products.py 
@router.post(
    "",
    response_model=Product,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
    summary="Create a product",
)
def create_product(payload: ProductCreate) -> Product:
    """Create and return a new product.

    Args:
        payload: Product creation payload.

    Returns:
        The created product.
    """
    return service.create(payload)

docs.title: get_product(product_id)
docs.text:
Return a product by identifier.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default product_id int

Requested product identifier.

required

Returns:

Type Description Product

The matching product.

Raises:

Type Description HTTPException

Raised with status 404 when the product does not exist.

Source code in src\inventory_api\routers\products.py 
@router.get(
    "/{product_id}",
    response_model=Product,
    summary="Get one product",
)
def get_product(product_id: int) -> Product:
    """Return a product by identifier.

    Args:
        product_id: Requested product identifier.

    Returns:
        The matching product.

    Raises:
        HTTPException: Raised with status 404 when the product does not exist.
    """
    product = service.get_by_id(product_id)
    if product is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"Product {product_id} not found",
        )
    return product

docs.title: list_products()
docs.text:
Return all products.

Returns:

Type Description list[Product]

The list of products currently available.

Source code in src\inventory_api\routers\products.py 
@router.get(
    "",
    response_model=list[Product],
    summary="List products",
)
def list_products() -> list[Product]:
    """Return all products.

    Returns:
        The list of products currently available.
    """
    return service.list_all()

docs.title: inventory_api.main
docs.text: FastAPI application entry point.

docs.title: read_root()
docs.text:
Return a simple health message.

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, str]

A dictionary containing the application status.

Source code in src\inventory_api\main.py 
@app.get("/", tags=["health"])
def read_root() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return a simple health message.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the application status.
    """
    return {"status": "ok"}
